signal.raw_content:
EMA Quarterly Report: European Generic Medicines Market — Q1 2026

The European Medicines Agency (EMA) today published its Q1 2026 report on the European generic medicines market, covering pricing trends, market authorizations, and access considerations.

KEY FINDINGS:

Generic Penetration: Generic medicines accounted for 53% of prescription volumes across EU member states in Q1 2026, up from 51% in Q1 2025. Germany (68%), Netherlands (62%), and Poland (71%) had the highest generic penetration rates.

Pricing Pressures: Average generic medicine prices declined 4.2% year-over-year in Q1 2026, driven by increased competition in cardiovascular, diabetes, and respiratory therapeutic categories. Price pressure is most intense in highly-contested markets with 5+ generic competitors.

New Authorizations: EMA issued 47 new generic medicine marketing authorizations in Q1 2026, spanning 31 active substance categories. Biosimilar authorizations numbered 8 in the quarter.

Shortage Status: The EMA shortage task force identified 12 medicines at risk of shortage in Q1, primarily affecting older antibiotics and certain cardiovascular agents. None of the at-risk medicines are related to the supply chains monitored in this report.

REGULATORY UPDATES: The European Solidarity Mechanism for medicine shortages issued two cross-border supply facilitation decisions in Q1. The EU Pharmaceutical Legislation revision is proceeding through co-decision with publication expected in late 2026.

Note: This report covers the EU generic medicines market broadly and does not contain information about specific pharmaceutical starting material supply chains or individual CDMO operations.
